description: Add PIN recovery failsafe to the Desktop (Electron) parental system. Generate a recovery code at first PIN set, store its hash, and allow the parent to reset their PIN using the recovery code if forgotten. This addresses the critical gap where a forgotten PIN locks out the parent permanently.
steps: Modify `desktop/electron/services/parental.js`: (a) Add a `generateRecoveryCode()` function that produces a human-readable 12-character alphanumeric code (e.g. 3 groups of 4: XXXX-XXXX-XXXX). (b) Add a `recoveryHash` field to the store defaults (null until first PIN set). (c) Modify the lazy seeding in `ensureSeeded()` so that when the demo PIN '1234' is seeded, a recovery code is also generated, hashed, and stored. The raw recovery code for the seeded demo is '1234-1234-1234' (matching the demo PIN spirit - easy to find in docs). (d) Add `getRecoveryCode()` - generates a new recovery code, hashes+stores it, returns the raw code. Called once when parent first changes from default PIN. (e) Add `resetPinWithRecovery(recoveryCode, newPin)` - verifies the recovery code hash, and if valid resets the PIN and generates+returns a new recovery code. (f) Export the new functions.

Modify `desktop/electron/main.js`: Add new IPC channels - `'lp:recovery:status'` (returns whether recovery is configured and whether PIN is still default), `'lp:recovery:generate'` (generates and returns the recovery code - curator-only), and `'lp:recovery:reset'` (takes recoveryCode + newPin, resets PIN - this one should be accessible from BOTH windows since a locked-out parent needs it).
Modify `desktop/electron/preload-curator.js`: Expose `getRecoveryStatus()`, `generateRecoveryCode()`, and `resetPinWithRecovery(code, newPin)` on the `window.launchpad` bridge.
Modify `desktop/electron/preload.js`: Expose `resetPinWithRecovery(code, newPin)` on the child window bridge too (so the locked-out parent can recover from the PIN gate overlay).

Modify `desktop/src/apps/Parental.jsx`: (a) After the existing PIN change card, add a 'Wiederherstellungscode' (Recovery Code) section. Show a button 'Code anzeigen' that calls `generateRecoveryCode()` and displays it with a warning to write it down. (b) If `pinIsDefault` is still true after a PIN change, automatically show the recovery code once with a prominent notice.

Add recovery flow to the child-window PIN gate (the overlay shown when parent tries to open Familienzentrale): below the PIN input, add a 'PIN vergessen?' link that reveals a recovery code input field. On submit, call `resetPinWithRecovery(code, newPin)` and show appropriate success/failure.

Add unit tests in `desktop/electron/services/parental.test.js`: test that `generateRecoveryCode()` returns a 14-char string (XXXX-XXXX-XXXX), that `resetPinWithRecovery` works with correct code, fails with wrong code, and that a new recovery code is returned on successful reset.
acceptance_criteria: A recovery code is generated when PIN is first changed from default
resetPinWithRecovery(code, newPin) correctly resets the PIN when given the valid recovery code
resetPinWithRecovery fails gracefully with an invalid code
The recovery code UI is accessible from both the curator settings and the child-window PIN gate
All existing tests still pass plus new recovery tests pass
The recovery code is never stored in plaintext - only its hash
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/LAUNCHPAD/desktop && npm test` and confirm all tests pass including new recovery tests
findings: All 50 tests pass (6 new recovery tests). Build succeeds. setPin now returns {ok:true, recoveryCode:'...'} on first change from default, or boolean true on subsequent changes - callers in Parental.jsx updated to handle both. The electron-store persists to disk during tests so recovery store-backed tests work without mocking. Recovery code uses a 32-char alphabet (no I/O/0/1) for human readability.
